sub_topics.title: Masena (Situations)
competences: Bape bupanduluzi bwamasena. (Give descriptions of situations)

instructional_blocks.hook: Mwaswaigwa mweenzu kwenu wakazyila kucisi cimbi mpoonya uyanda kuswaya meebeebe ajanika kubusena nkomukkala. Kamutaninga mutola uyanda kuti mumupandulwide kusyoonto biyo kujatikizya busena oobo. Inga mwamupandulwida buti busena oobo?
teacher_steps: Ambila basicikolo kuti ciiyo ncakwaamba biyo mabala abelesyegwa nobapandulula masena
Penzya mabala abelesyegwa kupandulula masena atala (cipati, kasyoonto, cilamfwu, cifwaafwi)
Tondezya kuti mabala aayo aakonzya kubelesyegwa kupandulula zyintu, masena, bantu nokuba cimbi naa kuli
Gamba basicikolo kusansaula mabala aabelesyegwa kupandulula masena
Yungizya basicikolo kuzuzikizya kupandulula masena aatobela zyintu zyilabidwe
Gamba basicikolo kuzunyaula mabala aakonzyeka kubelekela mucipani
learner_steps: Swiilila mwiiyi nokwaamba mabala abelesyegwa kupandulula masena
Kwaamba busena bwazintu zyindene-indene zyili mukkalasize akunze aakkalasize
instructional_blocks.short_notes: This activity helps learners understand and use descriptive words (adjectives) to describe situations, places, people and things. Focus on words like cipati (big), kasyoonto (small), cilamfwu (long), cifwaafwi (short).

instructional_blocks.hook: Inga mwaambaula buti cintu cisansaidwe mumunzi?
teacher_steps: Penzya cintu cisansaidwe mumunzi atala (ng'anda, mizeezo, citabwa, muti, cikuta)
Gamba basicikolo kusansaula cintu eeco atobela zyintu zyabeneseesegwa
Yungizya basicikolo kupandulula cintu eeco aabelesya mabala aayandisya
Bedelesya cibalo cisansaidwe mukubuzya basicikolo kuti kapandulule busena bwazintu zyilabidwe
learner_steps: Sansaula cintu cisansaidwe mumunzi kutobela zyintu zyabeneseesegwa
Pandulula cintu eeco aabelesya mabala aatobela
Pandulula busena bwazintu zyilabidwe zyaambwa amwiiyi
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Learners identify and describe common village items using appropriate descriptive words.

sub_topics.short_notes: Kupandulula masena (Describing situations) helps learners develop vocabulary and communication skills by using descriptive words to talk about their environment, people, and things around them.

questions: Observe learners as they describe situations using appropriate words
Check if learners can identify descriptive words
Ask learners to describe objects around them
Assess learners' ability to use descriptive words in sentences

sub_topics.title: Zyintu (Things)
competences: Bape bupanduluzi bwazyintu. (Give descriptions of things)

instructional_blocks.hook: Busena bwabulongo buli buti kumweenzu kwenu?
teacher_steps: Penzya zyintu zyiindene-indene zyili mumweenzu (matabeela, zyigalilo, zyanwa, zyinlya)
Gamba basicikolo kusansaula zyintu eezyo kutobela bupanduluzi
Yungizya basicikolo kupandulula zyintu eezyo aabelesya mabala aayandisya
Bedelesya zyipikicca zyazyintu nokubuzya basicikolo kuti kapandulule
learner_steps: Pandulula zyintu eezyo aabelesya mabala aatobela
instructional_blocks.short_notes: This activity focuses on describing household items and everyday objects using descriptive vocabulary.

sub_topics.short_notes: Kupandulula zyintu helps learners expand their vocabulary by describing objects in their environment using size, color, shape and other attributes.

questions: Can learners describe objects using appropriate words?
Do learners understand size and color words?
Can learners identify objects based on descriptions?

sub_topics.title: Tusobano (Riddles)

teacher_steps: Penzya tusobano twimbi nokutondezya mbotuyungizya
Gamba basicikolo kuyungizya tusobano
Yungizya basicikolo kulemba tusobano twaabo
Bedelesya micito yakubeela nokubuzya tusobano
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Tusobano (riddles) help develop critical thinking, listening skills and cultural knowledge. Learners learn traditional riddles and create their own.

sub_topics.short_notes: Tusobano are traditional Chitonga riddles that play an important role in oral literature and cultural transmission. They develop language skills and logical thinking.

questions: Can learners create their own riddles?
Do learners understand the structure of riddles?

sub_topics.title: Tulabi a twaanga mulaka (Traditional stories)
competences: Swiilila tulabi twaanga mulaka. (Listen to traditional stories)
Pandulula zyakacitika mutulabi. (Describe what happened in stories)

teacher_steps: Ambaula labi liimwi lyaanga mulaka
Gamba basicikolo kuswiilila kapati
Yungizya basicikolo kupandulula zyakacitika mulabi
Bedelesya mibuzyo yamulabi nokugamba basicikolo kupandulula
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Traditional stories (tulabi) are important for cultural education, moral lessons, and language development. Learners listen, comprehend and retell stories.

sub_topics.short_notes: Tulabi a twaanga mulaka (traditional stories) preserve cultural values, teach moral lessons and develop listening and speaking skills.

questions: Can learners retell a story they heard?
Do learners understand the moral lesson?
Can learners answer questions about the story?

sub_topics.title: Twaanga mulaka (Traditional expressions)
competences: Belesya twaanga mulaka mumasena aayandisya. (Use traditional expressions in appropriate situations)

teacher_steps: Penzya twaanga twaanga mulaka (zyikumpanano, zyipozyo, nzyotumpela)
Tondezya mbotwabelesyegwa mumasena aayandisya
Gamba basicikolo kupilika abelesya twaanga mutomene
Yungizya basicikolo kubelesya twaanga twaanga mulaka
instructional_blocks.short_notes: Traditional expressions include greetings, proverbs, idioms and respectful language that are essential for cultural communication.

sub_topics.short_notes: Kwaamba twaanga mulaka preserves cultural identity and teaches appropriate communication in different social contexts.

questions: Do learners understand when to use respectful language?
